Today I walk outside at a relaxed pace. The sun shines on the green grass and a small bird sings cheerfully by the open window.
After breakfast I put my cup in the dishwasher and open the curtains. The street is quiet and the morning air feels fresh.
Tomorrow we are taking the train to a small town near the coast. I have packed a warm coat, a bottle of water and a good book.
The cat is sleeping comfortably on the sofa. When I call softly, she opens one eye and stretches her paws.
In the kitchen I slice two apples and place them in a blue bowl. Then I add yoghurt, oats and a little cinnamon.
A gentle rain taps against the window while I answer a few messages. In the distance, a car drives slowly through the wet street.
This afternoon I am meeting a friend at the library. We will look for a travel guide and drink coffee in the café next door.
The garden gate squeaks when the wind moves it. I close it carefully and carry the empty flowerpots into the shed.
Before dinner I take a short walk around the neighbourhood. Children are playing outside and someone is watering the plants.
On the table lies a notebook with a green pencil. I write down three ideas so I will remember them tomorrow morning.
The bus arrives a few minutes late, but everyone waits patiently. I find a seat by the window and watch the shops pass by.
We choose a quiet spot beneath the large tree. There we eat grapes and listen to the soft sound of the wind.
I turn on the small lamp beside the chair and unfold the newspaper. The room is warm and the clock ticks steadily.
At the market I buy fresh bread, tomatoes and a bunch of yellow flowers. The vendor places everything carefully in my bag.
A bicycle bell rings behind me on the narrow path. I step aside, smile at the rider and continue towards the bridge.
The kettle clicks off as the water begins to boil. I pour it into my mug and let the tea rest for a few minutes.
This evening the sky is clear enough to see several bright stars. I stand outside briefly and enjoy the cool, still air.
I tidy the desk, close the laptop and put the papers in a drawer. Everything is ready for a calm start tomorrow.
Before going to sleep I read one more chapter. Then I put the book away and switch off the small light.
A rabbit hops through the grass and stops beside a flower. Its long ears move when it hears a quiet sound.
